Kept as adopted, canonical object corrected to include the getTravelTime() comparison so the field list matches all cited lines exactly.

adjudications.basis: Reread Order.java:79 (PAID default), OrderOtherServiceImpl.java:128/277/292/368/400 (all enum-getCode gated writes/comparisons), OrderOtherController.java:106-109 and OrderOtherServiceImpl.java:333-340 (modifyOrder(int status) passes the raw path-variable int through, bypassing the enum). Pass-a's extra clause about a QueryInfo state=-1 'sentinel for no filter' is NOT supported: grep shows qi.getState() is read exactly once (service:156), gated by the isEnableStateQuery() boolean, and state is never compared to -1 anywhere in the reviewed files, so -1 has no downstream gating effect. Merged pass-a+pass-b into one canonical atom that keeps the modifyOrder passthrough exception (true, grounded in controller.java:109) and drops the unsupported QueryInfo -1 claim; QueryInfo.java:70 ref dropped accordingly.

canonicalAtom.subject: ctx:order-other-service-order-lifecycle
canonicalAtom.object: section=order-status-code: int status is set via OrderStatus enum getCode() accessors (PAID/CANCEL/CHANGE/NOTPAID/COLLECTED) at every gated write site in this chunk, except modifyOrder (controller+service), which passes an arbitrary caller-supplied int status straight through with no enum validation

adjudications.key: semantic|other.entity.QueryInfo|semantic|meansInUse|int state field set to -1 by disableStateQuery to select the no-state-filter branch downstream
adjudications.basis: Reread QueryInfo.java:68-71 (disableStateQuery sets state=-1) and grep for getState() usage: the only read is OrderOtherServiceImpl.java:156, gated entirely by qi.isEnableStateQuery() (never by comparing state to -1). No code in the reviewed files branches on state==-1, so the claimed downstream 'selects the no-state-filter branch' effect does not exist; the actual no-filter gate is the (separately buggy, see pass-b's disablestatequery-also-clears-traveldateflag row) enableStateQuery boolean, not the state value. Dropped as an unsupported causal claim.

adjudications.key: semantic|other.service.OrderOtherServiceImpl|semantic|meansInUse|int 0 selects the not-found-or-rejected branch of the Response envelope, data is typically null or a sentinel value
adjudications.basis: Reread OrderOtherServiceImpl.java:69-242 (status-0 sites) -- lines 73/82/96/125/138/242 all `new Response<>(0,...,null-or-echoed-input)` on not-found/duplicate/rejected paths. Pass-a split this into a separate status-0 atom; the status-1 half (next row) and pass-b's combined row describe the same bivalent envelope convention, so merged into one atom covering both branches, matching how the analogous NotificationServiceImpl boolean-branch atom was already kept as one fact.
